explain.rule: The past continuous (was/were + verb-ing) describes an action that was in progress at a specific moment in the past. Three main uses: (1) An ongoing action interrupted by a shorter past simple action — I was cooking when the phone rang. (2) Two parallel ongoing actions at the same time — While she was reading, he was watching TV. (3) Setting the scene or background in a story — It was raining and the streets were empty.
explain.why_matters: Past continuous makes your storytelling rich and natural. Native speakers use it constantly when telling stories and explaining what was happening when something occurred. Without it, your English sounds flat.
explain.hebrew_note: Hebrew uses past simple for all of these situations. 'בישלתי כשהטלפון צלצל' — both verbs in Hebrew past simple. In English you must distinguish: cooking was in progress (was cooking, past continuous) when the phone rang (completed interruption, past simple). This distinction is a clear B1 skill.
explain.tip: The key pattern: WHILE/WHEN + was/were + -ing (background) + past simple (event/interruption). The -ing action was already happening. The past simple action happened at a point during it. Also: use WHILE to connect two simultaneous -ing actions: While she was cooking, he was setting the table. Never use stative verbs (know, believe, want, love) in continuous forms.

form.positive: I/he/she/it + was + verb-ing | you/we/they + were + verb-ing
form.negative: I/he/she/it + wasn't + verb-ing | you/we/they + weren't + verb-ing
form.question: Was + I/he/she/it + verb-ing? | Were + you/we/they + verb-ing?

good_examples.sentence: I was watching TV when she called.
good_examples.audio_text: I was watching TV when she called.

good_examples.sentence: While he was driving, she was looking at the map.
good_examples.audio_text: While he was driving, she was looking at the map.
good_examples.note: Two parallel actions at the same time

good_examples.sentence: It was snowing when we arrived.
good_examples.audio_text: It was snowing when we arrived.

good_examples.sentence: What were you doing at 8 o'clock last night?
good_examples.audio_text: What were you doing at 8 o'clock last night?
good_examples.note: Asking about an ongoing action at a specific past time

good_examples.sentence: I was living in Paris when I met my partner.
good_examples.audio_text: I was living in Paris when I met my partner.

good_examples.sentence: Sorry I missed your call — I was having a shower.
good_examples.audio_text: Sorry I missed your call — I was having a shower.
good_examples.note: Explanation for what was in progress

good_examples.sentence: The children were sleeping when we got home.
good_examples.audio_text: The children were sleeping when we got home.

good_examples.sentence: While I was waiting, I noticed something strange.
good_examples.audio_text: While I was waiting, I noticed something strange.
good_examples.note: While + past continuous + past simple event during

good_examples.sentence: It was a typical morning: people were rushing to work and the cafés were filling up.
good_examples.note: Scene-setting description of a past situation

good_examples.sentence: She wasn't paying attention when the teacher explained.
good_examples.audio_text: She wasn't paying attention when the teacher explained.
good_examples.note: Negative past continuous + interrupted by past simple

bad_examples.wrong: I cooked when the phone rang.
bad_examples.correct: I was cooking when the phone rang.
bad_examples.reason: Cooking was already in progress when the phone rang — past continuous needed for the background action.

bad_examples.wrong: It was raining when I was leaving.
bad_examples.correct: It was raining when I left.
bad_examples.reason: Raining = ongoing background (past continuous). Leaving = completed interruption (past simple).

bad_examples.wrong: What did you do at 9pm yesterday? (asking about ongoing action)
bad_examples.correct: What were you doing at 9pm yesterday?
bad_examples.reason: Asking about an action in progress at a specific time → past continuous.
Know is a stative verb — it does not use -ing forms in any tense.

bad_examples.wrong: While I walked home, I found a wallet.
bad_examples.correct: While I was walking home, I found a wallet.
bad_examples.reason: Walking = ongoing action during which the finding happened → past continuous.

exercises.question: I ___ (watch) TV when the lights went out.
exercises.hint: Ongoing action interrupted → past continuous
exercises.explanation: was watching (ongoing) + went out (interruption).

exercises.question: While she ___ (read), he ___ (cook) dinner.
exercises.hint: Two parallel ongoing actions → both past continuous
exercises.explanation: Two simultaneous ongoing actions → both past continuous.

exercises.question: It ___ (snow) when we ___ (arrive).
exercises.hint: Snow = ongoing background. Arrive = completed action.
exercises.explanation: was snowing (background) + arrived (completed).

exercises.question: What ___ you ___ (do) at midnight last night?
exercises.hint: Action in progress at a specific past time → past continuous
exercises.explanation: What were you doing? — ongoing at midnight.

exercises.question: She ___ (not / pay) attention when the teacher ___ (explain) the rule.
exercises.hint: Not paying = ongoing. Explained = completed event.
exercises.explanation: wasn't paying attention (ongoing) + explained (completed).

exercises.question: Sorry I missed your call — I ___ (have) a shower.
exercises.hint: What was in progress when the call came in
exercises.explanation: was having a shower — in progress.

exercises.question: I ___ (live) in London when I ___ (meet) my best friend.
exercises.hint: Living = ongoing background. Met = specific event.
exercises.explanation: was living (background) + met (specific event).

exercises.question: ___ they ___ (wait) long when you arrived?
exercises.hint: Question about ongoing action in the past
exercises.explanation: Were they waiting? — ongoing question.

exercises.question: The sun ___ (shine) and the birds ___ (sing) when I woke up.
exercises.hint: Scene-setting in the past — both background actions
exercises.explanation: Scene-setting: was shining, were singing — both background.

exercises.question: He ___ (drive) too fast when the accident ___ (happen).
exercises.hint: Ongoing dangerous action + the event
exercises.explanation: was driving (ongoing) + happened (event).

exercises.question: While I ___ (walk) home, I ___ (find) a wallet on the pavement.
exercises.hint: Walking = ongoing. Finding = completed event during the walk.
exercises.explanation: was walking (ongoing) + found (event during).

exercises.question: She ___ (not / expect) to get the job but they called her that afternoon.
exercises.hint: Ongoing mental state at that time
exercises.explanation: wasn't expecting — her state of mind at that time.

exercises.question: I ___ to music when he knocked on the door.
exercises.hint: Ongoing action interrupted
exercises.explanation: was listening (ongoing) + knocked (interruption).

exercises.question: While I was cooking, the children ___ their homework.
exercises.hint: Parallel action — both in progress at the same time
exercises.explanation: Two simultaneous actions → both past continuous.

exercises.question: She ___ a book about grammar when I saw her.
exercises.hint: Ongoing action at the time of seeing her
exercises.explanation: was reading — in progress when I saw her.

exercises.question: She ___ the answer — she just pretended not to.
exercises.hint: know = stative verb. Does it take -ing?
exercises.explanation: Know is stative — never use -ing. She knew the answer.

exercises.question: The children ___ so loudly that I couldn't concentrate.
exercises.hint: Ongoing noisy activity in the background
exercises.explanation: were talking — ongoing background action.

exercises.question: Which sentence uses past continuous correctly?
exercises.answer: I was having dinner when she arrived.
exercises.hint: Which shows a background action interrupted by an event?
exercises.explanation: was having dinner (ongoing) + arrived (interruption) = correct past continuous use.
distractors: I having dinner when she arrived.
I was have dinner when she arrived.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'I cooked when the phone rang.'
exercises.answer: I was cooking when the phone rang.
exercises.hint: Which action was already in progress?
exercises.explanation: Cooking was in progress → past continuous. Rang = interruption → past simple.
exercises.hebrew_note: פעולה שהייתה בתהליך ונקטעה = was/were + ing

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'What did you do at 9pm last night? (about ongoing action)'
exercises.answer: What were you doing at 9pm last night?
exercises.hint: Asking about an action in progress at a specific time
exercises.explanation: Ongoing action at a specific time → past continuous.

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'It was raining when I was leaving the house.'
exercises.answer: It was raining when I left the house.
exercises.hint: The rain was ongoing — but leaving is the interruption
exercises.explanation: Raining = background (past continuous). Left = completed action/interruption (past simple).

exercises.question: Find and fix: 'She was knowing the answer all along.'
exercises.answer: She knew the answer all along.
exercises.hint: know = stative verb — no -ing forms
exercises.explanation: Stative verbs never use -ing: know, believe, understand, love, hate, want.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: I was watching TV when she called.
exercises.hint: Past continuous (background) + when + past simple (interruption)
exercises.explanation: was watching (ongoing) + when + called (interruption).

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: While he was driving, she was reading the map.
exercises.hint: While + past continuous + past continuous
exercises.explanation: Two simultaneous ongoing actions.

exercises.question: Put the words in the correct order:
exercises.answer: What were you doing at noon yesterday?
exercises.hint: Question about ongoing action at a specific past time
exercises.explanation: What were you doing at noon? — ongoing action at specific time.

speak.sentence: I was having breakfast when I heard the news.
What were you doing when the earthquake happened?
While she was waiting for the bus, she saw a car accident.
chunks: While she was waiting for the bus,

speak.sentence: It was raining heavily and everyone was rushing to find shelter.
chunks: and everyone was rushing to find shelter.

speak.sentence: I was living in a tiny flat when I started my first real job.
chunks: I was living in a tiny flat
when I started my first real job.

speak.sentence: Sorry, were you trying to say something? I wasn't listening.
chunks: Sorry, were you trying to say something?
speak.phonetic_hints: 'Trying' = TRY-ing. 'Listening' = LIS-un-ing — middle syllable very short.

speak.sentence: While the manager was talking, everyone was checking their phones.
The whole city was still sleeping when the news broke.
chunks: The whole city was still sleeping
speak.phonetic_hints: 'Whole' = HOHL — W is silent. 'Sleeping' = SLEEP-ing. 'Broke' = BROHK.
